spec_match.rationale: Uses real ::part(input):focus-visible and ::part(input):hover host-page rules against a genuine shadow-DOM custom element. The visible focus ring is genuinely drawn by the host CSS not by JS.

interactive.rationale: Real form fields the user can tab through, plus a pre-130-mode toggle that demolishes the host CSS and wires JS focus listeners to simulate the workaround world. Both branches are testable in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a <form-field> with a part='input' surfacing :focus-visible from the host page — page delivers exactly that, plus a comparable pre-130 mode.

edge_cases.rationale: Two modes are good but the rejected-list mixes truly-rejected pseudos (the pre-130 list) with the now-OK list; no probe distinguishes what would still fail in Chrome 130 (combinators, :has).

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5078764563611648 — WRONG ID. The feature index uses 5195333643272192. The 5078764563611648 link will resolve to a different feature and this is the self-heal anchor, so the slug-recovery script could mis-recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: All CSS variables, mode-btn overrides background and color together, contrast on ivory/black combinations is fine for AA.

openQuestions.title: Fix the chromestatus feature ID
openQuestions.detail: The references link points to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5078764563611648 but the feature index uses 5195333643272192. CLAUDE.md rule #3 calls this out specifically as the self-heal anchor — a wrong ID here means /tmp/fix-slugs.py would rename the folder against the wrong feature on the next slug recovery pass. Replace with the correct ID.

openQuestions.title: Probe combinators and :has() rejection
openQuestions.detail: Add a third panel that runs CSS.supports() against still-rejected chains (form-field::part(input) > .x, form-field::part(input):has(.y), form-field::part(input):nth-child(2)) so the visitor leaves with a clear mental model of the new boundary, not just confirmation of what works.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate :focus-within at the label part
openQuestions.detail: The rejected-list teases form-field::part(label):focus-within but the page never applies it. Wire it up so the label restyles when the input is focused — that's a richer demo than just outlining the input itself.

summary: Genuinely good interactive demo with a real shadow-DOM component and a working pre-130 fallback. Marred only by the wrong chromestatus feature ID in references — a copy-paste error from a sibling page that needs fixing because the ID is also the slug-recovery anchor.
